Lorvenian School of Witchcraft and Wizardry

In the heart of the world of magic, the Lorvenian School of Witchcraft and Wizardry stands as the centerpiece for magical knowledge. It has spires reaching toward the heavens, large, ornate and dawning halls and uexplainable architecture only explained by the destruction of physics itself. Founded nearly 1,000 years ago by the Archmage Lorvenius Altharen, the school was established to unify the realms under the light of magical understanding and to nurture those with an affinity for the arcane arts. Its curriculum spans spellcraft, potion-making, enchantment, magical creature studies, and the ancient rites of magic.

Students from all corners of the realms journey to Lorvenia, drawn by its reputation as a sanctuary of learning and exploration. Here, they learn the differences between White Magic and Black Magic:

  • White Magic is a craft of healing, protection, and creation, fostering harmony between magical forces and nature. It is the magic of life.

  • Black Magic, however, is a forbidden art—a force of destruction, manipulation, and domination. It consumes those who wield it, corrupting their bodies, minds, and souls. Where White Magic gives, Black Magic takes.

The Dark Awakening

Centuries ago, during a time of peace, a former alumnus of Lorvenia, Aldred Malcair, unearthed the first spell of Black Magic—a dark incantation named Occurs Vactor. Aldred’s discovery marked the beginning of the period now known as the Dark Awakening.

The spell was unlike any other: it summoned the north winds, carrying dark, swirling clouds that descended upon a teacher, draining their life force and leaving behind a desiccated, hollow husk. Witnesses recounted how Aldred's body twisted and changed as he cast the spell. His limbs elongated grotesquely, his eyes burned with an unnatural green flame, and his skin became pallid and veined, as though death itself had claimed him. His voice, once human, turned guttural and otherworldly.

This vile act sent shockwaves through Lorvenia. The council of professors, led by the Headmistress Elarion Farsight, confronted Aldred, resulting in a clash that devastated the school's east wing. Though the professors managed to drive him away, Aldred fled into the northern wastelands, taking with him the forbidden knowledge of Black Magic.
